fundamentals.name: Spotify Technology S.A.
fundamentals.longBusinessSummary: Spotify Technology S.A., together with its subsidiaries, provides audio streaming subscription services worldwide. It operates in two segments, Premium and Ad-Supported. The Premium segment offers online and offline streaming access to its catalog of music and podcasts, including video, lossless music, and audiobooks in select markets through subscription offerings primarily sold directly to end users and partners. The Ad-Supported segment provides limited on-demand online access to its catalog of music and online and offline access to its catalog of podcasts on computers, tablets, mobile devices, and other smart devices. The company also offers sales, distribution and marketing, contract research and development, and customer and other support services. Spotify Technology S.A. was incorporated in 2006 and is headquartered in Stockholm, Sweden.

judgment.summary: Spotify shows strong growth in earnings and revenue, with healthy margins and a solid balance sheet, though it carries significant debt relative to equity.
judgment.earningsRecap: Last quarter beat EPS estimates by 18.0%; next earnings date is 2026-07-28.
positives: Earnings growth 222.4% YoY and quarterly earnings growth 220.4% indicate strong profitability improvement.
Operating margin 15.8% and Profit margin 15.4% are robust.
Return on equity 38.0% and Return on assets 11.6% demonstrate efficient use of capital.
Free cash flow $707.75M TTM is positive and substantial.
Forward P/E 28.46x is reasonable given the high growth estimates.
negatives: Debt/Equity ratio 5.94 is very high, indicating significant leverage.
Revenue growth 8.2% YoY is modest compared to earnings growth.
PEG ratio 2.17 suggests the stock may be somewhat overvalued relative to its expected earnings growth.

news.paragraph: Spotify (SPOT) has seen a mix of news, with a Motley Fool article highlighting a "troubling trend for Bitcoin" and other PR Newswire releases detailing unrelated initiatives like a cancer support program and a retail concept launch. The company's own name, SPOT, was also mentioned in a local business award. There is no direct, recent news impacting Spotify's core business operations or financial outlook.
